steps.title: Use elimination to isolate x
steps.content:
The $y$ coefficients are $-21$ and $14$; their LCM is 42. Multiply equation 1 by 2 and equation 2 by 3 to make the $y$ coefficients opposites:
$$2(22x - 21y) = 2(43) \quad \Rightarrow \quad 44x - 42y = 86$$
$$3(5x + 14y) = 3(59) \quad \Rightarrow \quad 15x + 42y = 177$$
Add the equations to eliminate $y$:
$$59x = 263$$

steps.title: Solve for x and identify p
steps.content:
Divide both sides by 59:
$$x = \frac{263}{59}$$
Since the solution is $\left(\frac{p}{59}, \frac{w}{826}\right)$, we have $p = 263$.
